babyDevelopment: The baby is starting to inhale and exhale small amounts of amniotic fluid, essential for lung development. Eyes are beginning to open, and the iris is becoming pigmented. The baby's response to light becomes more pronounced.
bodyChanges: You may experience Braxton Hicks contractions more frequently. Your back might feel more strain as the uterus continues to expand. Some women notice 'pregnancy-induced carpal tunnel' syndrome symptoms becoming more apparent.
tips: Practice pelvic floor exercises (Kegels) to support your bladder and pelvic organs
Sleep with a pillow between your knees to align your hips and reduce back pain
Keep up with regular, light walking to improve circulation

do: Hydrate well to manage fluid retention
Discuss any unusual symptoms during your prenatal visits
dont: Overheat in hot showers or baths
Lift heavy objects that put strain on your back

travelAdvisory.note: Safe to travel; ensure you take frequent breaks to walk.

milestoneDescription: Baby's eyes open for the first time, and pupil response to light begins.
